top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: Diplomacy dominates: the US and Iran signed a 14-point deal to wind down their war, starting a 60-day countdown, and the first oil tankers crossed the Strait of Hormuz as Tehran agreed to dilute its enriched uranium. But the violence hasn't stopped elsewhere — Ukraine launched its largest-ever attack on Moscow, and Gaza's health ministry says deaths since the ceasefire have passed 1,000. On the economy, a new-look US Fed signalled possible rate hikes, sending stocks down, while Apple warned prices will rise as the AI boom pushes up chip costs.

stories.headline: US and Iran sign 14-point deal to end war; first tankers cross Hormuz
stories.summary: Trump and Iran's president signed a 14-point agreement intended to end the conflict, opening a 60-day countdown on a fragile truce, with Washington saying Iran will dilute its enriched uranium. The Straits Times reported the first oil tankers have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, though core sticking points remain unresolved and implementation talks are still planned in Switzerland.
stories.source: BBC, Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times

stories.headline: US Fed signals possible rate hikes, raises inflation forecast
stories.summary: At new chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting in charge, the US Federal Reserve lifted its inflation forecast and signalled rate hikes could be on the horizon. US stocks fell and the dollar rallied in response, per CNA.

stories.headline: Ukraine launches largest attack on Moscow since start of the war
stories.summary: Moscow was hit by the largest Ukrainian attack since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion, with huge blasts shaking a refinery, the BBC and The Straits Times reported. President Zelensky said 'Moscow will burn' if Russian strikes on Ukraine continue.

stories.headline: South Korea launches company to manage US$350 billion US investment pledge
stories.summary: South Korea set up a dedicated company to manage its US$350 billion investment pledge to the United States, CNA reported, as Seoul moves to implement commitments made under its trade understanding with Washington.

stories.headline: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
stories.summary: Apple said it will raise prices as surging AI demand drives up the cost of chips, the BBC reported, a sign of how the AI build-out is feeding through to consumer electronics.

stories.headline: Gaza death toll since ceasefire passes 1,000, health ministry says
stories.summary: The death toll from Israeli fire in Gaza since the ceasefire has passed 1,000, according to the territory's health ministry, The Straits Times reported. The figure is attributed to the Gaza health ministry and has not been independently verified.

stories.headline: Modi reports 'significant progress' in US-India trade talks after Trump meeting
stories.summary: India's PM Narendra Modi said US-India trade negotiations made significant progress following a meeting with Trump at the G7, CNA reported, while the BBC reported Trump said he will visit India as the once-frosty relationship thaws.

stories.headline: Hegseth tells NATO US will review force presence in Europe
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told NATO that Washington will review its force presence in Europe, while hailing progress at the alliance, CNA reported.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A busy local day: Singapore regained the top spot in the IMD world competitiveness ranking, MAS added crypto exchange Bybit to its investor alert list, and IMDA launched a S$48 million scheme to help media firms create digital content and adopt AI. On the ground, 22 people were taken to hospital after a multi-vehicle pile-up in Tuas, a Pasir Panjang nursing home had its licence revoked over safety lapses, and police warned of malware scams that have cost Android-using seniors at least $69,000. Lighter chatter centred on a man jailed for sending SM Lee a doctored 'MBS on fire' photo. Weather: thundery showers, 25–34°C.

stories.headline: Singapore regains top spot in world competitiveness ranking
stories.summary: Singapore returned to first place in the latest IMD World Competitiveness Ranking, with Asian economies putting up a strong showing overall, CNA reported.

stories.headline: MAS adds crypto exchange Bybit to investor alert list
stories.summary: The Monetary Authority of Singapore added Bybit, one of the world's largest crypto exchanges, to its investor alert list, warning that it is not regulated by MAS, The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: Singapore launches S$48m scheme to help media firms create digital content, adopt AI
stories.summary: IMDA unveiled a S$48 million, four-year programme to support Singapore media companies in producing digital content and experimenting with AI, CNA, The Straits Times and Mothership reported.
stories.source: Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times, Mothership

stories.headline: Singapore's 2027 public holidays released, with 5 long weekends
stories.summary: The Ministry of Manpower released the 2027 public holiday dates, which include five long weekends, CNA, The Straits Times and Mothership reported.
stories.source: Channel NewsAsia, The Straits Times, Mothership

stories.headline: Pasir Panjang nursing home has licence revoked over serious lapses
stories.summary: MOH is revoking the licence of the 45-bed Windsor Convalescent Home in Pasir Panjang from 30 Oct, citing serious lapses in safety and hygiene, CNA and The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: 22 people taken to hospital after multi-vehicle accident in Tuas
stories.summary: Twenty-two people were taken to hospital after a multi-vehicle accident in Tuas, The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: Nearly 8,000 BTO flats, including Bayshore and Tengah, to launch in October
stories.summary: HDB will launch nearly 8,000 BTO flats in October, with projects in Bayshore and Tengah among them, The Straits Times reported.

stories.headline: Android users lose at least $69,000 to malware scams targeting seniors
stories.summary: Android users have lost at least S$69,000 in under three months to malware scams targeting elderly victims, The Straits Times reported, the latest in a run of malware-based scam losses.

stories.headline: Man admits sending SM Lee a doctored photo of MBS on fire as 'prank'
stories.summary: A 35-year-old man admitted to sending SM Lee Hsien Loong's Facebook page a digitally altered image of Marina Bay Sands ablaze, which he described as a prank, and faces jail, CNA and Mothership reported.

stories.headline: Man jailed after returning to Redhill shop with 2 knives over a straw
stories.summary: A man was sentenced to six months' jail after returning to a Redhill shop armed with two knives because he was not given a straw, Mothership reported.

stories.headline: HDB-themed café Lou Shang to close, owner says they lost S$400,000
stories.summary: HDB-themed café Lou Shang at Prinsep Street will close on 14 Jul, with the owner saying the business lost S$400,000, Mothership reported. The loss figure is attributed to the owner.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: The Iran front is de-escalating fast: the US and Iran signed a 14-point deal opening a 60-day countdown, the first tankers have crossed the Strait of Hormuz, and Tehran agreed to dilute its enriched uranium — though implementation talks in Switzerland are still pending. Elsewhere the fighting intensified: Ukraine launched its largest-ever strike on Moscow and Zelensky warned 'Moscow will burn', while in the Levant Israel kept striking Lebanon despite US criticism and Gaza's health ministry says deaths since the ceasefire have passed 1,000.

theatres.name: US / Israel–Iran (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: De-escalating: 14-point US-Iran deal signed 18 Jun; 60-day countdown begins; first tankers cross Hormuz

timeline.event: Trump and Iran's president sign a 14-point agreement aiming to end the war, opening a 60-day countdown on a fragile truce.
US says Iran agrees to dilute its enriched uranium under the peace deal.
First oil tankers cross the Strait of Hormuz after the interim deal, though Israeli strikes stir doubt in Lebanon.
Germany deploys ships to the Red Sea for a possible Hormuz mission, its minister says.
Talks on implementing the US-Iran deal are still planned in Switzerland on Friday, Bern says.

theatres.name: Israel–Lebanon & Gaza
theatres.status: Active: Israel strikes Lebanon despite US criticism; Gaza ceasefire deaths pass 1,000

timeline.event: Israel launches fresh strikes on Lebanon despite criticism from Trump, the BBC reported.
Gaza's health ministry says the death toll from Israeli fire since the ceasefire has passed 1,000 (figure attributed to the ministry, not independently verified).

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine
theatres.status: Active: Ukraine strikes deep into Russia; largest attack on Moscow of the war

timeline.event: At the G7, Trump strikes a more supportive tone on Ukraine's war aims in talks involving Zelenskyy, CNA reported.
Russian attacks could cut Ukraine's grain exports by a third, officials say.
Ukraine launches its largest attack on Moscow since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion; huge blasts shake a refinery.
Zelensky says 'Moscow will burn' if Russian strikes on Ukraine continue.

outbreaks.label: Outbreaks
outbreaks.ai_summary: The dominant active outbreak is Bundibugyo Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, spilling into Uganda: WHO's latest update (13 Jun) reported 695 confirmed cases and 138 deaths across both countries, with cases climbing rapidly in DRC's Ituri province. Insecurity is hampering the response — armed men stormed a DRC hospital, leaving a six-year-old patient missing. The earlier cruise-ship hantavirus cluster (13 cases, 3 deaths) has had no new WHO update in three weeks and appears contained.

outbreaks.name: Bundibugyo Ebola — DR Congo & Uganda
outbreaks.status: Escalating; cases rising rapidly in DRC's Ituri province
outbreaks.scope_note: WHO-confirmed only, DRC + Uganda combined (DRC 676 cases/136 deaths as of 10 Jun; Uganda 19 cases/2 deaths as of 11 Jun). WHO DON607, 13 Jun 2026. ~37 recoveries reported. Newer field tallies may exceed this not-yet-DON-ratified figure.

stories.headline: WHO: Bundibugyo Ebola cases climb to 695 with 138 deaths in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: In its 13 June Disease Outbreak News, WHO reported 695 confirmed Bundibugyo virus cases (676 in DRC, 19 in Uganda) and 138 deaths, up sharply since the 8 June update partly due to scaled-up testing. The outbreak remains concentrated in DRC's Ituri province.

stories.headline: Armed men storm DR Congo hospital; six-year-old Ebola patient missing
stories.summary: A search is under way for a six-year-old Ebola patient after armed men stormed a hospital in DR Congo, the BBC reported, underscoring how insecurity is complicating the outbreak response.

stories.headline: 'I buried my parents one day after the other': Ebola mourners learn to grieve safely
stories.summary: The BBC reported on how families in DR Congo are learning to mourn safely amid the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak, as unsafe burials remain a key driver of transmission.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Vulnerabilities and active exploitation lead the cycle: F5 shipped out-of-band patches for critical NGINX flaws, Apple fixed a Beats Studio Buds eavesdropping bug, and CISA flagged a max-severity Joomla JCE flaw (CVE-2026-48907, CVSS 9.8) plus exploited Cisco SD-WAN Manager and LiteSpeed cPanel bugs. Supply-chain and AI-tooling risk is surging — a hijacked WordPress plugin update flow, malicious JetBrains plugins stealing AI API keys, 144 compromised Mastra npm packages, a LiteLLM gateway takeover and a one-click Microsoft 365 Copilot flaw. On the breach front, a FortiBleed leak exposed VPN credentials for ~73,000 Fortinet devices and Kodak confirmed a ShinyHunters breach.

stories.headline: ShapedPlugin update flow hacked to infect WordPress sites
stories.summary: Attackers hijacked the ShapedPlugin update flow to push malicious code to WordPress sites, BleepingComputer reported, the latest plugin-update supply-chain compromise to hit WordPress.

stories.headline: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices
stories.summary: A leak dubbed FortiBleed exposed Fortinet VPN credentials for around 73,000 devices, BleepingComputer reported, raising the risk of follow-on intrusions.

stories.headline: Apple fixes Beats Studio Buds flaw that let hackers spy on conversations
stories.summary: Apple patched a flaw in its Beats Studio Buds that could let attackers eavesdrop on conversations, BleepingComputer reported.

stories.headline: F5 issues out-of-band patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities
stories.summary: F5 released out-of-band patches for critical vulnerabilities in NGINX, BleepingComputer reported, urging administrators to update promptly.

stories.headline: Microsoft confirms RoguePlanet Defender zero-day, patch in development
stories.summary: Microsoft confirmed a zero-day dubbed RoguePlanet affecting Microsoft Defender and said a patch is in development, per THN and BleepingComputer.

stories.headline: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins steal AI API keys from developers
stories.summary: Malicious JetBrains Marketplace plugins were found stealing developers' AI API keys, with related Chrome extensions capturing chatbot conversations, BleepingComputer and THN reported.

stories.headline: 144 Mastra npm packages compromised via hijacked contributor account
stories.summary: Attackers compromised 144 Mastra npm packages after hijacking a contributor account, THN reported, the latest in a string of npm supply-chain attacks.

stories.headline: CISA warns of actively exploited Joomla JCE flaw allowing PHP code execution
stories.summary: CISA added a max-severity flaw in the Joomla Content Editor (CVE-2026-48907, CVSS 9.8) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch by Friday; it allows PHP code execution, per THN and BleepingComputer.
stories.source: The Hacker News, BleepingComputer, CISA KEV

stories.headline: Kodak confirms data breach claimed by ShinyHunters extortion gang
stories.summary: Kodak confirmed a data breach that the ShinyHunters extortion group had claimed, BleepingComputer reported.

stories.headline: Google Vertex AI SDK flaw let attackers hijack model uploads via bucket squatting
stories.summary: A flaw in the Google Vertex AI SDK could let attackers hijack model uploads through 'bucket squatting', THN reported, highlighting growing risks in AI/ML supply chains.

stories.headline: New Rokarolla Android malware steals PINs, SMS codes and crypto wallet funds
stories.summary: A new Android malware family dubbed Rokarolla steals device PINs, SMS one-time codes and drains crypto wallet funds, THN reported.

stories.headline: Attackers exploit three Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws, one patched last week
stories.summary: Threat actors are chaining three Fortinet FortiSandbox flaws, one of which was patched only last week, THN reported.

stories.headline: China-linked SprySOCKS backdoor expands to Windows with driver-based stealth
stories.summary: The China-linked SprySOCKS backdoor has been ported to Windows, adding driver-based stealth techniques, THN reported.

stories.headline: Cisco releases security updates for actively exploited SD-WAN Manager flaw
stories.summary: Cisco issued updates for an actively exploited path-traversal flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (CVE-2026-20262), now in CISA's KEV catalog, per THN.

stories.headline: CISA flags LiteSpeed cPanel plugin flaw exploited for root privilege escalation
stories.summary: CISA added a symlink-following flaw in the LiteSpeed cPanel plugin (CVE-2026-54420) to its KEV catalog, exploited for root privilege escalation, THN reported.

stories.headline: Chinese hackers abused Google Workspace rules to steal research and defense emails
stories.summary: A China-linked espionage group abused Google Workspace mail rules to siphon research and defense-related emails, THN reported.

stories.headline: LiteLLM vulnerability chain lets low-privilege users take over AI gateway servers
stories.summary: A vulnerability chain in BerriAI's LiteLLM (CVE-2026-42271, CVSS 8.8) allows low-privilege users to take over AI gateway servers via command injection; the CVE is in CISA's KEV catalog, per THN.
stories.source: The Hacker News, NVD, CISA KEV

stories.headline: One-click Microsoft 365 Copilot flaw could have let attackers steal emails, files and MFA codes
stories.summary: Researchers disclosed a one-click flaw in Microsoft 365 Copilot that could have let attackers steal emails, files and MFA codes, THN reported. Microsoft has since addressed the issue.
